title: My Aunt Enters the Town Dumpling Competition
title_zh: 阿姨參加鎮上水餃大賽

all.en: Every spring, our small town holds a dumpling-making competition at the community center.
all.zh: 每年春天，我們的小鎮都會在社區中心舉辦包餃子比賽。

all.en: For the past five years, my Aunt Lin had watched from the audience, quietly taking notes.
all.zh: 過去五年來，林阿姨都坐在觀眾席上靜靜地做筆記。

all.en: This year, she finally decided to sign up and compete against twenty other home cooks.
all.zh: 今年，她終於決定報名，與另外二十位家庭廚師一較高下。

all.en: Our whole family was both excited and a little nervous about her sudden bold decision.
all.zh: 全家人對她突如其來的大膽決定既興奮又有點緊張。

all.en: She practiced every weekend for two months, filling our freezer with experimental batches.
all.zh: 她連續兩個月每個週末都在練習，把我們的冷凍庫塞滿了實驗性的餃子。

all.en: I became her unofficial taste tester, eating dumplings until I dreamed about them at night.
all.zh: 我成了她非官方的試吃員，吃餃子吃到晚上做夢都會夢到。

all.en: Aunt Lin tried pork with leek, shrimp with chives, and even a daring pumpkin filling.
all.zh: 林阿姨試做了豬肉韭菜、蝦仁韭黃，甚至大膽嘗試了南瓜內餡。

all.en: She finally settled on a classic pork and cabbage recipe passed down by her own mother.
all.zh: 她最後選定了一道由她母親傳下來的經典豬肉高麗菜配方。

all.en: On competition day, the community center smelled wonderfully of garlic and steamed dough.
all.zh: 比賽當天，社區中心瀰漫著大蒜和蒸麵皮的美妙香氣。

all.en: Aunt Lin arrived early to set up her station and arrange her ingredients carefully.
all.zh: 林阿姨早早抵達，仔細擺設好攤位並整理食材。

all.en: Her hands shook slightly as she tied a clean white apron around her waist.
all.zh: 她繫上潔白的圍裙時，雙手微微顫抖。

all.en: The judges included a famous chef from Taipei and two respected restaurant owners.
all.zh: 評審包括一位來自台北的知名主廚和兩位備受敬重的餐廳老闆。

all.en: Each contestant had ninety minutes to prepare and cook fifty perfect dumplings.
all.zh: 每位參賽者有九十分鐘可以準備並煮好五十顆完美的餃子。

all.en: Aunt Lin began chopping cabbage with the steady rhythm she had practiced for weeks.
all.zh: 林阿姨以練習數週的穩定節奏開始切高麗菜。

all.en: Beside her, a younger contestant was already struggling with his rolling pin.
all.zh: 在她旁邊，一位較年輕的參賽者已經開始與擀麵棍奮鬥。

all.en: Aunt Lin offered him a kind smile and a brief tip about flouring the surface.
all.zh: 林阿姨給了他一個親切的微笑，並簡短建議他在桌面撒些麵粉。

all.en: Her own dough came together smoothly, soft and elastic in her experienced hands.
all.zh: 她自己的麵團在熟練的手中成型，柔軟而有彈性。

all.en: She folded each dumpling with eighteen tiny pleats, the way her mother had taught her.
all.zh: 她用母親教過的方式，為每顆餃子捏出十八道細小的褶子。

all.en: The whole family cheered quietly from behind the rope, careful not to distract her.
all.zh: 全家人在繩索後方輕聲為她加油，小心不要讓她分心。

all.en: With ten minutes left, she dropped the first batch into the boiling pot of water.
all.zh: 在剩下十分鐘時，她把第一批餃子放進滾水鍋中。

all.en: Steam rose around her face, and the kitchen lights seemed to glow more warmly.
all.zh: 蒸氣從她臉旁升起，廚房的燈光似乎也變得更加溫暖。

all.en: She plated the dumplings neatly, drizzling them with a homemade chili oil sauce.
all.zh: 她整齊地擺盤，淋上自製的辣油醬汁。

all.en: The judges took small bites, chewed thoughtfully, and made notes on their clipboards.
all.zh: 評審們小口品嘗，仔細咀嚼，並在板夾上寫下評語。

all.en: The famous Taipei chef raised his eyebrows after the first bite, clearly surprised.
all.zh: 那位台北知名主廚咬下第一口後挑起眉毛，明顯地感到驚訝。

all.en: He asked Aunt Lin where she had learned to balance the salt and sweetness so perfectly.
all.zh: 他問林阿姨在哪裡學會把鹹味和甜味平衡得如此完美。

all.en: She simply smiled and said her mother had been her only teacher.
all.zh: 她只是微笑著說，她的母親就是她唯一的老師。

all.en: When the results were announced, my heart was pounding so loudly I could barely hear.
all.zh: 宣布結果時，我的心跳聲大到我幾乎聽不見廣播。

all.en: Third place went to a young man with creative seafood dumplings, second to a longtime competitor.
all.zh: 第三名是一位做創意海鮮餃的年輕男子，第二名是一位多年的老參賽者。

all.en: Then the announcer called out Aunt Lin's name as the grand winner of the year.
all.zh: 接著廣播員念出林阿姨的名字，宣布她是當年的總冠軍。

all.en: She covered her mouth with both hands, eyes wide and shining with happy tears.
all.zh: 她雙手摀著嘴，眼睛瞪得大大的，閃爍著喜悅的淚光。

all.en: We rushed forward to hug her, nearly knocking over her carefully arranged plate.
all.zh: 我們衝上前擁抱她，差點撞翻她精心擺好的盤子。

all.en: The prize was a beautiful wooden plaque and a generous gift card to a local market.
all.zh: 獎品是一塊精美的木製獎牌和一張當地市場的豐厚禮券。

all.en: But Aunt Lin said the real prize was finally honoring her mother's recipe in public.
all.zh: 但林阿姨說，真正的獎賞是終於能在眾人面前發揚母親的食譜。

all.en: That night, she cooked dumplings for everyone at home and refused to let us help.
all.zh: 那天晚上，她為家中每個人煮餃子，並堅持不讓我們幫忙。

all.en: She said winning had reminded her that some old traditions still deserved a loud celebration.
all.zh: 她說獲勝讓她想起，有些古老的傳統依舊值得一場熱鬧的慶祝。

all.en: The next morning, a local newspaper reporter knocked on our door to interview Aunt Lin.
all.zh: 隔天一早，當地報社記者敲響了我們家的門想採訪林阿姨。

all.en: She invited him into the kitchen and offered him a plate of freshly steamed dumplings.
all.zh: 她請他到廚房，並端上一盤剛蒸好的餃子。

all.en: He asked if she would ever consider opening her own small dumpling stall in town.
all.zh: 他問她是否會考慮在鎮上開設自己的小型餃子攤。

all.en: Aunt Lin laughed and said she preferred teaching neighbors over running a busy business.
all.zh: 林阿姨笑著說，比起經營忙碌的生意，她更喜歡指導鄰居們做菜。

all.en: Within a week, several mothers from the neighborhood asked her to start a weekend class.
all.zh: 不到一週，社區裡好幾位媽媽便請她開設週末烹飪課。

all.en: She agreed to host one session every Saturday afternoon in our spacious old dining room.
all.zh: 她答應每週六下午在我們寬敞的老餐廳裡上一堂課。

all.en: The first lesson filled up in two days, with a waitlist almost as long as the class.
all.zh: 第一堂課兩天內就額滿，候補名單幾乎和正式學員一樣長。

all.en: Aunt Lin taught with patience, showing each student how to feel the dough's readiness.
all.zh: 林阿姨耐心地教導，示範每位學員如何感受麵團的最佳狀態。

all.en: By summer, her classes had spread to three nearby towns through cheerful word of mouth.
all.zh: 到了夏天，她的課程靠著愉快的口碑傳遍了附近三個鎮。

all.en: She often said her late mother was now smiling proudly somewhere beyond the kitchen window.
all.zh: 她常說，她已故的母親如今正在廚房窗外的某處驕傲地微笑著。

all.en: By autumn, Aunt Lin's weekend classes had become so popular that local newspapers ran another feature.
all.zh: 到了秋天，林阿姨的週末課程已紅到當地報紙再次推出專題報導。

all.en: A community center invited her to teach a charity workshop where all proceeds supported elderly meal programs.
all.zh: 一間社區中心邀請她開設慈善工作坊，所有收益都用於支援長者送餐方案。

all.en: She agreed at once and asked her students to volunteer as her cheerful kitchen helpers.
all.zh: 她立即答應，並邀請她的學生們擔任她開心的廚房助手。

all.en: They prepared over four hundred dumplings in a single afternoon for the community fundraising dinner.
all.zh: 他們在一個下午內準備了超過四百顆餃子，供社區募款晚宴使用。

all.en: Local restaurant owners attended the dinner and asked Aunt Lin to consult on their winter menus.
all.zh: 當地餐廳老闆們也參加了晚宴，並請林阿姨為他們的冬季菜單擔任顧問。

all.en: She politely declined paid consulting but offered free advice over tea whenever they visited her home.
all.zh: 她婉拒了有償顧問的工作，但只要他們到她家拜訪，她都樂於泡茶免費分享建議。

all.en: The famous Taipei chef returned a year later to film a short documentary about her dumpling journey.
all.zh: 那位台北知名主廚一年後又回來了，為她的餃子故事拍攝一部短紀錄片。

all.en: He praised her quiet integrity and called her a true keeper of small town culinary heritage.
all.zh: 他讚揚她那份靜謐的正直，並稱她為小鎮飲食傳統的真正守護者。

all.en: Aunt Lin watched the finished film with our entire family one rainy Saturday afternoon.
all.zh: 林阿姨在一個下雨的週六下午，與我們全家一起觀看了完成的影片。

all.en: She cried softly when her late mother's old recipe card appeared on screen for several long seconds.
all.zh: 當她已故母親的老食譜卡片在螢幕上停留好幾秒時，她輕聲地哭了。

all.en: After the documentary aired, viewers from across Taiwan mailed handwritten letters to Aunt Lin's home address.
all.zh: 紀錄片播出後，全台灣的觀眾紛紛把手寫信寄到林阿姨家的地址。

all.en: She read every letter at the dining table and replied to each one with a postcard from town.
all.zh: 她在餐桌上讀完每一封信，並用一張小鎮的明信片親自回覆每一位來信者。

all.en: Her nephew offered to digitize all her recipes into a small cookbook app for free use anywhere.
all.zh: 她的姪子主動提議把她所有食譜數位化，做成一個小型免費的食譜 App。

all.en: Aunt Lin insisted that the app must include videos of hands kneading dough, not just photos of ingredients.
all.zh: 林阿姨堅持這個 App 必須包含揉麵的雙手影片，而不只是食材的照片。

all.en: She filmed the videos slowly, narrating in both Mandarin and her hometown Taiwanese dialect.
all.zh: 她緩慢地錄製影片，並用中文和家鄉的台語兩種語言旁白。

all.en: Three culinary schools requested permission to use her videos in their introductory dumpling classes.
all.zh: 三所餐飲學校請求授權，希望在他們的入門包餃子課程中使用她的影片。

all.en: Aunt Lin agreed without any fee, asking only that students respect their own grandmothers' kitchens.
all.zh: 林阿姨毫不收費地同意，只要求學生們尊重自己家中阿嬤的廚房。

all.en: On the second anniversary of her competition win, the whole town hosted a community dumpling festival.
all.zh: 在她比賽奪冠兩週年的那天，整個小鎮舉辦了一場社區包餃子嘉年華。

all.en: Hundreds of neighbors gathered with their own family recipes, sharing dough, fillings, and old stories.
all.zh: 數百位鄰居帶著各自的家傳食譜聚在一起，分享麵團、餡料和老故事。

all.en: Aunt Lin walked among the tables in her white apron, eyes shining like the lanterns above her head.
all.zh: 林阿姨穿著她那件白圍裙在桌間穿梭，眼睛閃耀得像頭頂的燈籠。

all.en: Every spring our town holds a dumpling contest at the community center.
all.zh: 每年春天，我們鎮上都會在社區活動中心辦水餃大賽。

all.en: For five years my Aunt Lin only watched, quietly taking notes.
all.zh: 整整五年，我阿姨林只在一旁看，默默做筆記。

all.en: This year she finally signed up against twenty other home cooks.
all.zh: 今年她終於報名，跟另外二十位家庭廚師同台較勁。

all.en: She practiced folding dumplings until her fingers ached every night.
all.zh: 她每晚練習包水餃，直到手指發痠。

all.en: Keep the filling juicy, but never let the skin tear open.
all.zh: 餡要多汁，但皮絕對不能破。

all.en: On contest day, the whole family came to cheer her on loudly.
all.zh: 比賽當天，全家人都來大聲為她加油。

all.en: She said the real prize was honoring her late mother's recipe.
all.zh: 她說真正的獎賞，是把已故母親的食譜發揚出去。

all.en: The judges praised how thin yet sturdy each dumpling skin was.
all.zh: 評審稱讚她每張餃子皮都又薄又有韌性。

all.en: Win or lose, she had already conquered her own quiet shyness.
all.zh: 不管輸贏，她都已經戰勝了自己那份沉默的羞怯。

all.en: She walked among the tables in her white apron, eyes shining brightly.
all.zh: 她穿著白圍裙在桌間穿梭，雙眼閃閃發亮。
